Title: Manipulation of the Expression of Tryptophan Decarboxylase Boosts Grain Functional Quality and Stress Resilience Capacity of Wheat

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cell wall polysaccharide architecture associated with root cell wall thickening
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens physical barrier contributing to cereal cyst nematode resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher TaXTH23 expression is associated with thicker root cell walls and enhanced CCN resistance in AevTDC1-OX wheat
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaXTH23 expression was increased in AevTDC1-OX roots. 2. AevTDC1-OX roots displayed significantly thicker cell walls. 3. The study proposes that TaXTH23-mediated wall modification contributes to improved CCN resistance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; TEM observation of root cell wall thickness; CCN resistance assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased in AevTDC1-OX root compared with non-transgenic wheat root
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for enhancing nematode resistance via strengthening root physical barriers

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool; directly initiates target-metabolite branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for grain functional quality and human health; also associated with stress resilience
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of tryptamine, serotonin, melatonin and other Trp-derived metabolites in wheat grains; enhances CCN resistance and drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of AevTDC1 in wheat markedly depleted grain tryptophan and strongly increased tryptamine, serotonin, N-hydroxytryptamine and melatonin. 2. AevTDC1-OX grains also accumulated more GABA, L-ascorbate and reduced glutathione, indicating improved functional quality. 3. AevTDC1-OX improved resistance to cereal cyst nematode and drought, with little effect on major yield traits. 4. Drought tolerance was associated with elevated ethylene biosynthesis/signaling and reduced stomatal aperture; CCN resistance was associated with increased TaXTH23 expression and thicker root cell walls.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in wheat; qPCR; targeted and widely targeted metabolomics; LC-MS/HPLC-MS quantification; drought assay; CCN resistance assay; stomatal SEM; TEM cell wall observation; transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as rate-limiting enzyme of tryptophan metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; cereal cyst nematode infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in overexpression lines Line2 and Line9; higher in Line9
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Widely targeted metabolomics (supporting tables S2, S3); comparative transcriptome analysis under drought (supporting tables S4 and related supplementary datasets)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slightly reduced plant height, biomass, and spikelet number per spike, but no significant impairment of grain number per spike, effective spike number per plant, grain size, or thousand grain weight
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising metabolic engineering and breeding target to improve wheat grain functional quality while simultaneously enhancing resistance to drought and cereal cyst nematode with minimal yield penalty
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species differs from applied species; heterologous expression from a wheat relative was used. The paper also links AevTDC1 to salicylic acid biosynthesis based partly on previous work, but the current study primarily validates effects on Trp-derived metabolites, ethylene-associated drought tolerance, and cell wall-related CCN resistance.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts S-adenosyl-L-methionine into ACC, the direct precursor of ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates stomatal aperture and contributes to drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher TaACS expression is associated with increased ACC content and improved drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaACS expression was elevated in AevTDC1-OX wheat and induced by drought. 2. Its promoter contains W-box motifs bound/activated by TaWRKY65. 3. Silencing TaWRKY65 reduced TaACS expression, lowered ACC, and decreased drought tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; ACC quantification by LC-MS/MS; promoter motif analysis; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; VIGS of upstream regulator
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key ethylene biosynthesis gene; key control point supported
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by drought and elevated in AevTDC1-OX lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by drought and elevated in AevTDC1-OX Line9 relative to non-transgenic wheat
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native TaACS promoter in regulatory assays; 35S promoter used for effector constructs in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptome analysis under well-watered, mild dehydration and severe dehydration conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream engineering target or marker for improving drought tolerance via ethylene-mediated stomatal regulation; may be combined with AevTDC1 or TaWRKY65
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct core experimental focus as downstream target of TaWRKY65 in drought-response module.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor with W-box-binding activity
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ethylene biosynthesis controlling stomatal aperture and drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TaACS expression by directly binding W-box motif(s) in the TaACS promoter
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes drought tolerance through stomatal regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates ACC/ethylene biosynthesis and drought tolerance in wheat
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaWRKY65 expression was higher in AevTDC1-OX wheat and induced by drought stress. 2. Silencing TaWRKY65 reduced TaACS expression and endogenous ACC content. 3. TaWRKY65 directly bound the TaACS promoter in yeast one-hybrid and activated TaACS promoter activity in dual-luciferase assays. 4. TaWRKY65 silencing reduced drought survival, demonstrating positive regulation of drought tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator linking AevTDC1/drought cues to ethylene biosynthesis and drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; BSMV-VIGS gene silencing; ACC quantification; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; drought survival assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated by drought; higher in AevTDC1-OX background
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by drought; nearly threefold higher before drought in AevTDC1-OX than non-transgenic wheat
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TaACS native promoter in Y1H/LUC reporter; 35S promoter for TaWRKY65 effector in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptome analysis under drought conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing compromises drought tolerance; no additional tradeoff reported for enhanced expression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulatory target for improving drought tolerance through ethylene biosynthesis with potential finer control than broad upstream manipulation of tryptophan metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper proposes TaWRKY65 as a novel transcriptional activator of ethylene biosynthesis in wheat drought tolerance.
